channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Young Climate Prize 2026: Global Accelerator Empowering Under-25 Climate Innovators to Lead Real-World Climate Solutions
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The climate crisis continues to demand urgent, innovative, and locally grounded solutions. One of the most impactful global initiatives supporting young changemakers is the Young Climate Prize, an international mentorship and award accelerator program led by The World Around. This program identifies, supports, and amplifies young climate leaders under the age of 25 who are […]

The post Young Climate Prize 2026: Global Accelerator Empowering Under-25 Climate Innovators to Lead Real-World Climate Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The climate crisis continues to demand urgent, innovative, and locally grounded solutions. One of the most impactful global initiatives supporting young changemakers is the Young Climate Prize, an international mentorship and award accelerator program led by The World Around.

This program identifies, supports, and amplifies young climate leaders under the age of 25 who are actively developing and implementing practical solutions to environmental challenges in their communities. Since its launch in 2022, the initiative has become a powerful platform for climate innovation, collaboration, and global visibility.

What Is the Young Climate Prize?

The Young Climate Prize is a global mentorship and award accelerator designed to support young innovators working on the frontlines of the climate crisis. It focuses on individuals who are not only aware of environmental challenges but are actively building and implementing solutions.

The program brings together:

  • Mentorship from global experts
  • International recognition and visibility
  • Support for scaling climate solutions
  • A strong global network of peers and leaders

It is built on the belief that meaningful climate action is already happening at the community level and that young people are at the center of this transformation.

Purpose and Vision of the Program

The core mission of the Young Climate Prize is to:

  1. Identify young climate leaders working in real-world conditions
  2. Support locally rooted and scalable climate solutions
  3. Provide mentorship from global leaders in design, architecture, science, and sustainability
  4. Amplify grassroots innovation on a global stage
  5. Strengthen collaboration between young innovators worldwide

The program emphasizes action over theory, prioritizing projects that already exist and demonstrate measurable impact.

Who Can Apply?

The Young Climate Prize targets young innovators who are:

  • Actively working on climate-related projects
  • Developing real-world, community-based solutions
  • Demonstrating innovation and adaptability
  • Focused on sustainability, resilience, or environmental justice

Applicants typically include:

  • Environmental entrepreneurs
  • Community climate activists
  • Designers and engineers
  • Researchers and innovators
  • Grassroots organizers

Key Areas of Innovation Supported

The program supports a wide range of climate solutions, including but not limited to:

  • Waste management and circular economy systems
  • Renewable and alternative fuel development
  • Sustainable materials and material innovation
  • Biodiversity conservation initiatives
  • Climate education and awareness projects
  • Urban sustainability and infrastructure solutions
  • Community-based adaptation strategies
  • Creative environmental solutions, including unconventional approaches like ecological storytelling and citizen science

Impact and Achievements Since 2022

Since its inception, the Young Climate Prize has:

  • Supported 50 young innovators
  • Represented participants from 35 countries
  • Funded and mentored projects addressing global climate challenges
  • Amplified solutions that are both locally rooted and globally scalable

These projects demonstrate that climate solutions are not limited to large institutions but are also being developed at grassroots level by young leaders.

Mentorship and Global Network (Cycle 03)

One of the strongest components of the program is its mentorship network. Participants gain access to leading global experts across multiple disciplines, including:

  • Architecture and design leaders
  • Environmental scientists
  • Curators and cultural theorists
  • Climate storytellers and communicators

Notable mentors include:

  • Paola Antonelli (Museums and design innovation)
  • Bruce Mau (Design and systemic transformation)
  • Mariam Issoufou (Architecture and sustainability)
  • Alice Rawsthorn (Design and social impact)
  • Douglas Cardinal (Indigenous architecture and sustainability)

This mentorship helps participants refine their ideas, scale their projects, and connect with global opportunities.

Benefits of the Young Climate Prize

Selected participants receive:

  • High-level mentorship from global experts
  • International exposure and recognition
  • Opportunities to scale climate solutions
  • Access to a global network of innovators
  • Skill-building through workshops and masterclasses
  • Support in developing long-term impact strategies
  • A platform to present work at global forums

How to Apply or Nominate

Interested applicants can:

  • Apply directly through the official Young Climate Prize platform
  • Be nominated by mentors, organizations, or peers
  • Submit their active climate projects for consideration

Applicants are encouraged to ensure their projects are:

  • Already in progress
  • Community-driven
  • Designed for real-world implementation
  • Scalable across regions or contexts

Deadline Information

No fixed application deadline has been publicly stated. Applicants are encouraged to apply ahead of the next selection cycle when applications open.

Why the Young Climate Prize Matters

The climate crisis requires immediate, localized, and innovative responses. This program stands out because it:

  • Focuses on real, existing solutions rather than theoretical ideas
  • Elevates youth leadership in climate action
  • Encourages interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Bridges local innovation with global platforms
  • Builds long-term capacity for environmental change

It is more than an award—it is an accelerator for global climate leadership.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Young Climate Prize 2026: Global Accelerator Empowering Under-25 Climate Innovators to Lead Real-World Climate Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Predoctoral Research Fellowship in Economics at the University of Zurich (2026–2028): A Premier Opportunity in Macroeconomics & Labor Markets
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The Department of Economics at the University of Zurich (UZH) is offering an exceptional Predoctoral Research Fellowship for aspiring economists seeking hands-on experience in high-level academic research. This full-time, in-person role provides a unique pathway into top-tier PhD programs and advanced research careers in economics. Starting as early as June 1, 2026 (flexible) and lasting […]

The post Predoctoral Research Fellowship in Economics at the University of Zurich (2026–2028): A Premier Opportunity in Macroeconomics & Labor Markets appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Department of Economics at the University of Zurich (UZH) is offering an exceptional Predoctoral Research Fellowship for aspiring economists seeking hands-on experience in high-level academic research. This full-time, in-person role provides a unique pathway into top-tier PhD programs and advanced research careers in economics.

Starting as early as June 1, 2026 (flexible) and lasting 1–2 years (2 years preferred), this fellowship is designed to immerse candidates in cutting-edge research focused on labor economics and macroeconomics, particularly addressing gender disparities in labor markets.

Why This Fellowship Stands Out

This predoctoral fellowship is not just a job—it is a career-launching platform. Fellows will work closely with Prof. Andreas I. Mueller, gaining mentorship and direct exposure to frontier research.

Key Highlights

  • Full-time, salaried position with competitive pay and benefits
  • 70% research assistance and 30% structured learning
  • Direct mentorship with regular one-on-one guidance
  • Access to seminars, workshops, and global research networks
  • Strong track record of fellows entering top PhD programs in Europe and the U.S.

Research Focus Areas

The fellowship centers on understanding persistent gender gaps in:

  • Wages
  • Labor force participation
  • Employment outcomes

Core Research Themes

  • Life-cycle career dynamics
  • Family formation and labor participation
  • Institutional and policy impacts across countries

Data Sources Used

  • Administrative wage and employment records (Switzerland & Europe)
  • Survey datasets such as:
    • CPS (Current Population Survey)
    • EU-LFS (European Labour Force Survey)
  • Cross-country macroeconomic panel data

Your Responsibilities as a Predoctoral Fellow

Fellows are actively involved in all phases of research. Key responsibilities include:

1. Data Management

  • Collecting, cleaning, and linking large datasets
  • Managing administrative and survey micro-data

2. Quantitative Analysis

  • Conducting econometric and statistical analyses using:
    • Stata
    • Python
    • R
    • Matlab

3. Model Development

  • Assisting in building and calibrating structural economic models

4. Research Support

  • Preparing results for:
    • Academic papers
    • Presentations
    • Referee reports

5. Academic Contribution

  • Conducting literature reviews
  • Supporting project coordination and research management

Eligibility Requirements

Required Qualifications

Applicants must have:

  • A Master’s degree (or exceptional Bachelor’s) in Economics or a related quantitative field
  • Strong knowledge of:
    • Microeconomics
    • Macroeconomics
    • Econometrics
    • Statistics
  • Experience using Stata and LaTeX
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities
  • Strong English communication skills (German not required)
  • Demonstrated interest in labor and macroeconomics

Preferred Qualifications

  • Programming skills in Python, R, or Matlab
  • Experience with large datasets (administrative or survey)
  • Familiarity with structural or quantitative economic models

Application Requirements

To apply, candidates must submit:

  1. Motivation Letter
    • Explain interest in the project and relevant experience
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  3. Academic Transcripts
  4. Two Letters of Recommendation
    • Preferably from research supervisors
  5. Writing Sample (Optional but Recommended)

CLICK HERE TO APPLY

For inquiries: olga.ring@econ.uzh.ch

What You Will Gain

This fellowship offers unmatched exposure to academic research and career development:

  • Practical experience in empirical macroeconomic research
  • Close collaboration with leading economists
  • Access to international academic networks
  • Opportunities to attend conferences and workshops
  • Strong preparation for competitive PhD programs

Inclusivity and Global Access

  • Open to EU and non-EU applicants
  • Work permit assistance provided
  • Additional support available for:
    • Applicants from low- and middle-income countries
    • Refugees and asylum seekers

These candidates are encouraged to apply via the UZH Pre-Doctoral Program Global Talent Common Application.

Deadline

No specific application deadline is stated. However, early application is strongly recommended due to the competitive nature of the program and rolling consideration.

Final Thoughts

If you are an aspiring economist looking to bridge the gap between academic training and high-impact research, this Predoctoral Research Fellowship at the University of Zurich is a rare and valuable opportunity. With world-class mentorship, access to rich datasets, and a strong academic network, this role positions you for success in top global PhD programs and research careers.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Predoctoral Research Fellowship in Economics at the University of Zurich (2026–2028): A Premier Opportunity in Macroeconomics & Labor Markets appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Green Microbiology Summit 2026: Global Hybrid Conference Launching a New Scientific Frontier in Sustainability, Climate, and Microbial Innovation
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The Green Microbiology Summit 2026 is set to become a landmark global event, marking the official launch of a transformative scientific field at the intersection of microbial science, sustainability, and planetary resilience. Scheduled to take place from July 15–18, 2026, this hybrid global summit will convene a diverse network of researchers, innovators, policymakers, educators, and […]

The post Green Microbiology Summit 2026: Global Hybrid Conference Launching a New Scientific Frontier in Sustainability, Climate, and Microbial Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Green Microbiology Summit 2026 is set to become a landmark global event, marking the official launch of a transformative scientific field at the intersection of microbial science, sustainability, and planetary resilience. Scheduled to take place from July 15–18, 2026, this hybrid global summit will convene a diverse network of researchers, innovators, policymakers, educators, and industry leaders to shape the future of Green Microbiology.

Designed as more than just a conference, this summit represents a defining moment in scientific collaboration and innovation—positioning microbial science as a powerful tool for addressing some of the world’s most pressing environmental and development challenges.

What is Green Microbiology?

Green Microbiology is an emerging interdisciplinary field focused on leveraging microbial science to address global sustainability challenges. It integrates biological systems with environmental and technological innovation to drive:

  • Sustainable food systems
  • Clean water access and sanitation
  • Renewable energy solutions
  • Climate resilience and ecological restoration
  • Public and environmental health
  • Artificial intelligence-driven biological discovery

Rather than being limited to traditional microbiology, this field emphasizes real-world applications that support circular economies, reduce environmental impact, and enable global systems transformation.

Why the Green Microbiology Summit 2026 Matters

The 2026 summit is uniquely positioned as a field-launch event, not a routine academic gathering. It aims to:

  1. Define the Intellectual Foundations
    Establish the conceptual framework and direction of Green Microbiology as a discipline.
  2. Showcase Cutting-Edge Research
    Present emerging innovations across science, technology, and sustainability.
  3. Foster Global Collaboration
    Connect multidisciplinary stakeholders from academia, policy, and industry.
  4. Catalyze Leadership and Innovation
    Inspire a new generation of leaders to drive sustainable solutions globally.
  5. Launch a Global Scientific Community
    Introduce the Society for Green Microbiology, creating a long-term platform for collaboration and impact.

Summit Highlights

1. Global Participation

  • Engage with participants from across continents and disciplines
  • Connect institutions, practitioners, and innovators worldwide

2. Hybrid Access

  • Participate either in-person or virtually
  • Designed to ensure inclusivity and international accessibility

3. Founding Contributor Recognition

  • Attendees will be recognized as Founding Contributors to the Green Microbiology movement
  • Opportunity to shape the identity and future direction of the field

Core Focus Areas

The summit will explore six major thematic areas critical to global sustainability:

  1. Food Systems
    • Soil health, fermentation, food safety, and sustainable agriculture
  2. Water Security
    • Wastewater treatment, sanitation technologies, and microbial water purification
  3. Energy Transitions
    • Bioenergy, microbial fuel systems, and circular industrial processes
  4. Health
    • One Health approaches, diagnostics, and environmental health innovations
  5. Artificial Intelligence
    • AI-driven microbial research, predictive modelling, and data integration
  6. Climate
    • Carbon cycles, biodegradation, and climate adaptation strategies

Summit Program (July 15–18, 2026)

Day 1: The Global Launch

  • High-level keynote sessions
  • Vision-setting discussions defining the field

Day 2: Flagship Summit Day

  • Plenary sessions
  • Strategic global dialogues
  • Institutional engagement

Day 3: Scientific Sessions

  • Research presentations
  • Abstract-based technical sessions
  • Interdisciplinary knowledge exchange

Day 4: The Future of Green Microbiology

  • Policy discussions
  • Industry engagement
  • Roadmap development for the field

Society for Green Microbiology Launch

A major milestone of the summit is the official launch of the Society for Green Microbiology, a global platform dedicated to:

  • Advancing research and innovation
  • Promoting policy engagement and sustainability advocacy
  • Supporting education, training, and capacity building
  • Recognizing excellence in the field
  • Building a global network of contributors

All summit participants will be recognized as Founding Contributors to this international community.

Call for Abstracts

Researchers and professionals are invited to submit abstracts for presentation at the summit.

Key Details

  • Submission Deadline: June 15, 2026
  • Submission Fee: None
  • Presentation Mode: Virtual or in-person
  • Submission Email: hello@greenmicrobiology.org

Format Requirements

  • Maximum 300 words
  • English language only
  • Microsoft Word (.doc or .docx)
  • Times New Roman, 12 pt, single spacing

Required Materials

  • Abstract
  • Structured content based on submission guidelines

Why Submit?

  • Present at a global, field-defining summit
  • Gain international visibility and recognition
  • Contribute to shaping a new scientific discipline
  • Engage with global experts across sectors

Who Should Attend

The summit is open to a broad range of participants, including:

  • Researchers and scientists
  • Microbiologists and biotechnologists
  • Environmental and sustainability experts
  • Policymakers and development practitioners
  • Industry innovators and entrepreneurs
  • Academic institutions and educators

How to Participate

  • Submit abstracts via email: hello@greenmicrobiology.org
  • Attend virtually or in person
  • Engage in partnerships or collaborations

For inquiries, partnerships, or speaker opportunities, contact the summit team via the official email.

Final Note

The Green Microbiology Summit 2026 represents a historic opportunity to be part of a global movement redefining science for sustainability. By participating, attendees are not only contributing to knowledge exchange but also actively shaping solutions for food security, climate resilience, energy sustainability, and environmental health.

For more information about this conference visit HERE 

To discover more conference opportunities on OFY visit here 

The post Green Microbiology Summit 2026: Global Hybrid Conference Launching a New Scientific Frontier in Sustainability, Climate, and Microbial Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: BRIMM Economics Leadership Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): Study Economic Leadership in Mining
item.dc:creator: Mercy N. Atsuki
item.description:
The BRIMM Economics Leadership Scholarship 2026 is a fully funded opportunity designed to equip women and underrepresented professionals in the mining sector with advanced economic and leadership skills. This program is part of the BRIMM Executive Micro Certificate in Economic Leadership, an intensive educational experience created to shape the next generation of industry leaders. Offered […]

The post BRIMM Economics Leadership Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): Study Economic Leadership in Mining  appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The BRIMM Economics Leadership Scholarship 2026 is a fully funded opportunity designed to equip women and underrepresented professionals in the mining sector with advanced economic and leadership skills. This program is part of the BRIMM Executive Micro Certificate in Economic Leadership, an intensive educational experience created to shape the next generation of industry leaders.

Offered by the Bradshaw Research Institute for Minerals and Mining, this scholarship is funded by external partners who are committed to breaking barriers in education. The goal is to ensure that talented professionals—regardless of their location, gender, or financial situation—can access high-quality mining education and contribute to building a more inclusive and sustainable industry.

About the Program

The BRIMM Executive Micro Certificate in Economic Leadership is an advanced program that integrates mining expertise with business and economic strategy. It is specifically designed for professionals who want to strengthen their leadership capacity while addressing real-world challenges in the mining sector.

Participants will engage in a structured, multi-course learning experience delivered over a single academic term. The program combines theoretical knowledge with practical applications, ensuring that learners can directly implement their insights in their professional environments.

Program Structure and Timeline

The scholarship covers a single semester during the Fall 2026 term, running from September to December 2026. The program is divided into four progressive courses:

  • BRIMM 101: September 8 – September 24, 2026
  • BRIMM 102: October 6 – October 22, 2026
  • BRIMM 103: November 3 – November 19, 2026
  • BRIMM 104: December 1 – December 17, 2026

Each course builds on the previous one, creating a continuous learning journey. Participants are required to successfully complete each course before progressing to the next.

For a detailed breakdown of modules, topics, and curriculum design, explore the full program overview HERE

Scholarship Benefits

The BRIMM Economics Leadership Scholarship offers a comprehensive package designed to support both learning and career growth:

  • Full Tuition Waiver: Covers all program fees, making the opportunity accessible to high-potential candidates
  • Industry-Relevant Training: Focused on economic leadership within the mining sector
  • Professional Networking: Opportunities to connect with global industry leaders, mentors, and peers
  • Career Advancement: Prepares participants for leadership roles and greater influence within the mining industry

What You Will Learn

Participants will gain practical and strategic skills that are directly applicable to modern mining challenges, including:

  • Implementing advanced economic strategies to improve efficiency and profitability
  • Integrating sustainable practices to reduce environmental impact
  • Developing data-driven solutions to complex industry problems
  • Enhancing leadership and decision-making capabilities in a global context

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for the scholarship, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be actively involved in the mining industry, or clearly demonstrate intent to transition into it
  • Show willingness to learn and fully engage with the program
  • Demonstrate motivation to contribute to a better and more inclusive mining industry
  • Have functional English skills for online learning, presentations, and assignments
  • Possess reliable internet access capable of supporting streaming and coursework

Who Should Apply

This scholarship is ideal for:

  • Women in mining or aspiring to enter the industry
  • Individuals from underrepresented communities
  • Professionals from countries outside the OECD
  • Mid-career professionals seeking leadership and economic expertise
  • Changemakers passionate about sustainability, innovation, and inclusion

Selection is based on diversity, equity, and inclusion principles, including gender, country of origin, and the unique perspective each applicant brings to the industry.

Terms and Conditions

Applicants are strongly encouraged to review the scholarship terms carefully before applying:

  1. Duration of Award:
    The scholarship is valid only for the Fall 2026 semester (September–December 2026).
  2. Non-Transferability:
    Scholarships cannot be transferred to another individual or deferred to another term.
  3. Course Completion Requirement:
    Participants must pass each course to proceed to the next. Failure to meet course requirements will result in immediate termination of the scholarship and ineligibility for future opportunities.

For any inquiries regarding the scholarship terms, applicants can contact:
Dana Caudle via email at dana.caudle@ubc.ca

Application Process

The application process is currently open, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply within the official scholarship window.

  • Application Opens: April 28, 2026
  • Application Deadline: June 15, 2026

Applicants should ensure that all required information is submitted and that their application clearly highlights their experience, leadership potential, and commitment to transforming the mining industry.

Click HERE to start application process.

For more information about this opportunity, click HERE.

Why This Scholarship Matters

The mining sector is undergoing a significant transformation, with increasing emphasis on sustainability, innovation, and inclusivity. However, leadership within the industry still lacks sufficient diversity.

The BRIMM Economics Leadership Scholarship addresses this gap by:

  • Empowering underrepresented voices in mining
  • Promoting sustainable and responsible mining practices
  • Encouraging innovative and data-driven decision-making
  • Building a global network of forward-thinking leaders

This program is not just an academic opportunity—it is a catalyst for long-term industry change.

Discover more scholarship opportunities HERE.

The post BRIMM Economics Leadership Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): Study Economic Leadership in Mining  appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NIHR–Wellbeing of Women Doctoral Award 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Future Leaders in Women’s Health Research
item.dc:creator: Mercy N. Atsuki
item.description:
The NIHR–Wellbeing of Women Doctoral Award 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded PhD opportunity designed to support researchers who are passionate about advancing women’s health. This unique partnership between the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR) and Wellbeing of Women aims to develop the next generation of independent research leaders by funding impactful […]

The post NIHR–Wellbeing of Women Doctoral Award 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Future Leaders in Women’s Health Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The NIHR–Wellbeing of Women Doctoral Award 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded PhD opportunity designed to support researchers who are passionate about advancing women’s health. This unique partnership between the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR) and Wellbeing of Women aims to develop the next generation of independent research leaders by funding impactful and innovative doctoral studies.

This award is part of the NIHR Charity Partnership Programme, which brings together leading research funders and charitable organizations to strengthen collaboration and drive meaningful improvements in healthcare outcomes.

About the NIHR–Wellbeing of Women Doctoral Award

The Doctoral Award provides funding for individuals to undertake a PhD while conducting high-quality research that contributes to improving health systems, services, and outcomes—particularly in the area of women’s reproductive and gynaecological health.

Researchers selected for this award will benefit from being part of a vibrant and supportive research community, gaining exposure to cross-sector collaboration and access to valuable development opportunities.

Key Focus Areas

Wellbeing of Women is particularly interested in funding research that addresses critical issues in women’s health across the life course, including:

  • Pregnancy, birth, and postnatal care
  • Menstrual and gynaecological health
  • Gynaecological cancers
  • Fertility, contraception, and abortion care
  • Pelvic floor health
  • Menopause
  • Healthy ageing and long-term conditions
  • Sexual health

What the Doctoral Award Covers

Successful applicants will receive comprehensive funding to support their PhD journey, including:

  1. Full Salary Support
    • Covers the researcher’s salary with protected time dedicated to research.
  2. PhD Tuition Fees
    • Full coverage of academic fees required for the doctoral programme.
  3. Research Costs
    • Funding for all essential research-related expenses.
  4. Tailored Training and Development
    • A bespoke programme designed to meet individual career development needs.

Why Apply for This Award?

This Doctoral Award is more than just funding—it is a career-defining opportunity that enables researchers to:

  • Build a strong foundation as an independent research leader
  • Contribute to impactful, real-world health improvements
  • Collaborate across academic, clinical, and charitable sectors
  • Gain access to mentorship and professional development resources

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be based at an institution located in England
  • Demonstrate a role in or contribution to improving health and wellbeing, healthcare services, or social care
  • Propose research aligned with women’s reproductive or gynaecological health (for this partnership award)

Applicants are strongly encouraged to:

  • Review detailed eligibility requirements HERE.
  • Contact Wellbeing of Women to confirm alignment of their research topic if unsure

Application Process

To apply for the NIHR Charity Partnership Doctoral Award:

  1. Follow the standard NIHR application process HERE.
  2. Ensure compliance with both NIHR and Wellbeing of Women eligibility requirements
  3. Prepare and submit all required documentation before the deadline
  4. Click HERE to submit your application.

For assistance with your application:

  • Email: personal.awards@nihr.ac.uk
  • To learn more about this opportunity, click HERE.

For additional inquiries:

  • Email: research@wellbeingofwomen.org.uk
  • Phone: 020 3697 6346

Important Dates

  • Call Launch: 21 April 2026
  • Application Deadline: 23 June 2026 at 1:00 PM
  • Award Start Date: Between April and September 2027

The NIHR–Wellbeing of Women Doctoral Award 2026 is an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers dedicated to improving women’s health outcomes. With full financial support, structured development, and access to a collaborative research ecosystem, this programme empowers candidates to make a lasting impact while advancing their academic and professional careers.

Discover more PhD opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post NIHR–Wellbeing of Women Doctoral Award 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Future Leaders in Women’s Health Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Rothschild Foundation Grassroots Grants Fund 2026: Up to £5,000 Available for Community Organisations Tackling Inequality in Buckinghamshire
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
Community organisations working tirelessly to combat disadvantage and inequality in Buckinghamshire now have an opportunity to secure financial support through the Rothschild Foundation’s Grassroots Grants Fund. This funding initiative is designed to empower local organisations that are making a real difference in their communities, particularly in areas such as homelessness, financial hardship, mental health, and […]

The post Rothschild Foundation Grassroots Grants Fund 2026: Up to £5,000 Available for Community Organisations Tackling Inequality in Buckinghamshire appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Community organisations working tirelessly to combat disadvantage and inequality in Buckinghamshire now have an opportunity to secure financial support through the Rothschild Foundation’s Grassroots Grants Fund. This funding initiative is designed to empower local organisations that are making a real difference in their communities, particularly in areas such as homelessness, financial hardship, mental health, and disability support.

The Fund provides grants of up to £5,000 for one year and is specifically aimed at supporting incorporated grassroots organisations that may be small in scale but are delivering impactful community services. Whether funding is needed for operational activities, projects, or capital expenses, the programme seeks to strengthen organisations addressing urgent social challenges across Buckinghamshire.

About the Rothschild Foundation Grassroots Grants Fund

The Rothschild Foundation Grassroots Grants Fund exists to support local organisations working directly with disadvantaged communities. The programme focuses on grassroots organisations that understand local needs and provide practical solutions to improve lives.

The Foundation recognises that many smaller organisations often struggle to secure sustainable funding despite delivering essential community services. Through this grant programme, the Foundation aims to bridge that gap by helping organisations expand or sustain their work.

Funding is awarded to organisations that can clearly demonstrate both the need for their services and the impact their projects will have on beneficiaries.

Areas of Support

The grant programme prioritises organisations working in the following areas:

1. Homelessness

Funding is available for projects supporting individuals and families experiencing homelessness or housing insecurity. This may include emergency support services, accommodation assistance, outreach programmes, or rehabilitation initiatives.

2. Financial Hardship

Organisations helping people facing economic challenges may apply for funding. Eligible initiatives could include food support programmes, debt advice services, employment assistance, or financial education projects.

3. Physical and Learning Disabilities

Community groups supporting individuals with physical disabilities or learning difficulties are encouraged to apply. Funding may support accessibility initiatives, specialised programmes, inclusive community activities, or support services.

4. Mental Health and Wellbeing

Mental health services remain a major priority area for the Foundation. Grants may support counselling programmes, wellbeing workshops, peer support initiatives, community mental health projects, or other interventions promoting emotional wellbeing.

Funding Available

Successful applicants can receive:

  • Grants of up to £5,000
  • Funding for one year
  • Support for:
    • Project costs
    • Core operational costs
    • Capital costs

The Foundation also notes that where funding is requested solely for a capital project, applicants should demonstrate that additional funding sources are contributing toward the total project cost.

Reporting Requirements

Organisations awarded funding will be expected to submit a report at the end of the grant period. This report allows the Foundation to understand:

  • How the project was implemented
  • The impact achieved
  • Lessons learned
  • Community outcomes generated through the funding

This reporting process helps maintain transparency and ensures that grants continue supporting meaningful community change.

What the Fund Will Not Support

Certain activities and expenses are not eligible for funding under the programme. Applications involving the following will not be considered:

  • Grants benefiting individuals directly
  • Overseas travel
  • Religious or political promotion
  • Animal welfare activities
  • Campaigning work
  • Loan repayments
  • Activities already completed before funding approval
  • Statutory responsibilities of public bodies

Applicants should carefully review these exclusions before preparing their applications.

Who Can Apply?

The programme welcomes applications from a broad range of incorporated non-profit organisations, including:

  • Registered charities
  • Charitable Incorporated Organisations (CIOs)
  • Community Interest Companies (CICs limited by guarantee)
  • Incorporated community groups
  • Societies
  • Sports clubs

Importantly, newly registered organisations that do not yet have annual accounts may still apply, making the programme accessible to emerging grassroots initiatives.

To qualify, organisations must:

  • Have an annual income below £1 million
  • Be based in Buckinghamshire (excluding Milton Keynes), OR
  • Demonstrate that at least 60% of beneficiaries are based in Buckinghamshire

Organisations That Are Not Eligible

The following organisations are not eligible to apply:

  • Organisations already holding a Rothschild Foundation grant
  • Uniformed groups
  • Statutory organisations, including Town and Parish Councils
  • Organisations operating primarily in Milton Keynes
  • Organisations working mainly in neighbouring counties
  • For-profit businesses and commercial enterprises

Application Process

Applications must be submitted through the Rothschild Foundation’s funding portal. Applicants are encouraged to review the guidance provided before starting their application, especially first-time users of the system.

Applications can be submitted at any time during the year. However, submissions are reviewed four times annually according to the Foundation’s funding schedule.

Application Deadlines and Decision Periods

Application Deadline Applications Considered In
22nd May June
27th August October
18th November December
27th January March

Applicants will typically be informed of the funding decision within six weeks after each application deadline.

Why This Grant Matters

Grassroots organisations are often at the forefront of addressing social inequality and supporting vulnerable communities. However, limited funding can restrict their ability to maintain or expand critical services.

The Rothschild Foundation Grassroots Grants Fund provides an important opportunity for local organisations to:

  • Sustain vital community programmes
  • Expand services to reach more beneficiaries
  • Improve operational capacity
  • Strengthen long-term impact
  • Address urgent local social challenges

For many small organisations, a grant of up to £5,000 can make a significant difference in delivering meaningful community support.

Tips for a Strong Application

To improve the chances of success, applicants should:

  1. Clearly explain the problem their organisation is addressing.
  2. Provide evidence of community need and demand.
  3. Demonstrate measurable impact and expected outcomes.
  4. Explain how the funding will be used in detail.
  5. Show how the organisation is financially sustainable.
  6. Ensure eligibility criteria are fully met before submission.

Strong applications typically include real examples, data, testimonials, or evidence demonstrating the importance of the proposed work.

Contact Information

Applicants who need support or clarification regarding the programme may contact the funding team directly:

  • Email: grants@heartofbucks.org
  • Telephone: 01296 330134

Interested organisations are encouraged to begin preparing their applications early to ensure all required information is completed before the next funding deadline.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity click here.

Discover more grant opportunities on OFY here. 

The post Rothschild Foundation Grassroots Grants Fund 2026: Up to £5,000 Available for Community Organisations Tackling Inequality in Buckinghamshire appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AI Student Worker Opportunity at the LEGO Foundation 2026: Build Real-World AI Solutions in a Global Impact Organization
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The LEGO Foundation is offering an exciting opportunity for students passionate about artificial intelligence and its real-world application. The AI Student Worker role is designed for individuals eager to use cutting-edge AI tools to improve organizational efficiency while contributing to a mission focused on helping children thrive globally. This role is ideal for students who […]

The post AI Student Worker Opportunity at the LEGO Foundation 2026: Build Real-World AI Solutions in a Global Impact Organization appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The LEGO Foundation is offering an exciting opportunity for students passionate about artificial intelligence and its real-world application. The AI Student Worker role is designed for individuals eager to use cutting-edge AI tools to improve organizational efficiency while contributing to a mission focused on helping children thrive globally.

This role is ideal for students who are not just users of AI tools, but builders—individuals who can design, implement, and scale intelligent solutions that transform how work gets done.

About the Role

As an AI Student Worker, you will play a central role in driving AI-powered transformation within the Foundation. You will collaborate with teams across the organization to identify inefficiencies and implement practical AI-driven solutions.

The position is based in Billund, with flexible working hours ranging between 10–20 hours per week to accommodate your academic schedule.

Key Responsibilities

Your work will focus on delivering tangible improvements through AI and automation. Responsibilities include:

  1. Identifying Process Inefficiencies
    • Analyze workflows across teams
    • Detect repetitive or manual processes suitable for automation
  2. Building AI-Powered Solutions
    • Design and develop AI agents, copilots, and automated workflows
    • Utilize tools such as Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT, and Claude
  3. Prototyping and Testing
    • Develop internal tools such as chatbots, reporting systems, and knowledge assistants
    • Test and refine AI solutions based on feedback
  4. Scaling Solutions Across Teams
    • Expand successful prototypes into scalable, organization-wide solutions

What You Will Gain

This role offers hands-on experience in applying AI to solve real organizational challenges. Key benefits include:

  • Practical experience building and deploying AI solutions in a global organization
  • Exposure to real-world automation challenges and workflows
  • Opportunity to collaborate across diverse teams and departments
  • Development of problem-solving and execution skills in AI implementation
  • Contribution to a purpose-driven mission focused on children’s development

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for this opportunity, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Currently enrolled in a relevant degree program such as AI, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field
  • Have at least 1.5 years remaining in their studies

Required Skills and Experience

  • Hands-on experience with AI tools like Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT, Claude, or similar
  • Ability to build automations, workflows, or AI agents using APIs, scripting, or low-code platforms
  • Strong problem-solving skills, especially in structuring ambiguous challenges into actionable AI solutions
  • Willingness to experiment, iterate, and improve solutions continuously
  • Ability to work independently and take ownership of projects
  • Fluency in English and Danish (written and spoken)

Work Location and Flexibility

  • Based in Billund, Denmark
  • Shuttle bus service available from Aarhus and Vejle
  • Flexible working hours (10–20 hours per week)

Application Process

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and the position will close once a suitable candidate is selected.

Application Deadline: 17 May 2026

Application Requirements

  • Submit your application before the deadline HERE.
  • Include examples of AI solutions, automations, or agents you have built
  • To learn more about this opportunity, click HERE.

For inquiries, you can contact the Foundation via email at: people@legofoundation.com

About the LEGO Foundation

The LEGO Foundation is a Danish corporate foundation dedicated to improving children’s lives worldwide. Through partnerships and innovative initiatives, the Foundation works to create environments where children can grow, learn, and thrive. Its mission centers on championing the dignity of childhood and ensuring every child has the opportunity to reach their full potential.

Explore more job opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post AI Student Worker Opportunity at the LEGO Foundation 2026: Build Real-World AI Solutions in a Global Impact Organization appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Study Water & Sustainability in Europe: Fully & Partially Funded MSc Opportunities for Mexican Students at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
As global challenges like climate change, water scarcity, and environmental degradation intensify, the demand for skilled professionals in water management and sustainable development continues to grow. For Mexican students eager to contribute to meaningful global solutions, pursuing a Master of Science degree at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education presents a powerful opportunity. Located in […]

The post Study Water & Sustainability in Europe: Fully & Partially Funded MSc Opportunities for Mexican Students at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
As global challenges like climate change, water scarcity, and environmental degradation intensify, the demand for skilled professionals in water management and sustainable development continues to grow. For Mexican students eager to contribute to meaningful global solutions, pursuing a Master of Science degree at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education presents a powerful opportunity.

Located in the Netherlands, IHE Delft is globally recognized for its excellence in water education, research, and capacity development. The institute offers specialized MSc programmes designed to equip students with both practical and theoretical expertise to tackle real-world water-related challenges. Even more compelling, a wide range of fully and partially funded scholarships are available specifically for Mexican students.

About the MSc Programmes at IHE Delft

IHE Delft offers two primary postgraduate pathways tailored to different career goals:

1. MSc in Water and Sustainable Development

This programme is designed for students who want to apply their knowledge directly to solving pressing global water issues.

Key features include:

  • Focus on real-world water challenges and sustainable solutions
  • Courses in water management, governance, and environmental systems
  • Emphasis on practical, impact-driven learning
  • Ideal for professionals seeking careers in policy, consulting, NGOs, and development sectors

2. Research MSc in Water and Sustainable Development

This track is best suited for those interested in academic or research careers.

Highlights include:

  • Strong focus on scientific research and innovation
  • Preparation for PhD studies or advanced research roles
  • Opportunities to work on cutting-edge water-related research projects

Additional Opportunities

  • Joint MSc programmes with international partner universities
  • Exposure to interdisciplinary approaches combining science, policy, and engineering

Why Choose IHE Delft?

Studying at IHE Delft offers far more than academic advancement—it provides a transformative global experience.

Key Benefits:

  • World-Class Expertise: Learn from leading experts in water science, engineering, and policy
  • Global Learning Environment: Study alongside students from diverse cultural and professional backgrounds
  • Real-World Focus: Address urgent global issues such as climate resilience, sanitation, and water governance
  • Strong Professional Network: Build connections with international organizations, governments, and industry leaders
  • Career Advancement: Gain skills that are highly valued across global development sectors

Scholarships Available for Mexican Students

A variety of funding opportunities are available to support Mexican students in pursuing their studies. These scholarships may cover tuition fees, living expenses, or both.

Scholarships Specifically for Mexican Students

  1. SECIHTI: Posgrado en Ciencia y Humanidades en el Extranjero 2026
    • Deadline: 22 May 2026
    • Click here for more and to apply.

 

  1. Fundación Esposos Rodríguez
    • Deadline: 29 May 2026
    • Click here for more and to apply.

 

  1. Brockmann IHE Delft Scholarships
    • Deadline: 25 September 2026
    • Note: Applicable for MSc programmes starting in 2027/2028
    • Click here for more and to apply.

 A Gateway to Global Impact

Graduates from IHE Delft are equipped to become leaders in addressing some of the world’s most critical water and sustainability challenges. Whether working in government agencies, international NGOs, research institutions, or private sector organizations, alumni play a vital role in shaping a more sustainable future.This opportunity is especially valuable for Mexican students who want to gain international exposure while contributing solutions that can be applied both globally and locally.

For more global scholarship opportunities Click here

The post Study Water & Sustainability in Europe: Fully & Partially Funded MSc Opportunities for Mexican Students at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Think Visegrad Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Non-V4 Experts at IIR Prague
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Institute of International Relations Prague (IIR), in cooperation with the Think Visegrad platform, is pleased to announce the opening of applications for the Think Visegrad Fellowship 2026. This prestigious fellowship offers researchers, analysts, and policy experts from non-Visegrad countries a unique opportunity to engage with leading Central European think tanks, conduct high-impact policy research, and […]

The post Think Visegrad Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Non-V4 Experts at IIR Prague appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Institute of International Relations Prague (IIR), in cooperation with the Think Visegrad platform, is pleased to announce the opening of applications for the Think Visegrad Fellowship 2026. This prestigious fellowship offers researchers, analysts, and policy experts from non-Visegrad countries a unique opportunity to engage with leading Central European think tanks, conduct high-impact policy research, and contribute to strategic regional dialogue.

Think Visegrad is not merely a fellowship program — it is a structured think tank platform designed to foster meaningful dialogue on issues of strategic regional importance. Established in 2012 by eight think tanks from the Visegrad Group countries (Czech Republic, Hungary, Poland, and Slovakia), the platform has consistently provided actionable recommendations to V4 governments, the annual presidencies of the Group, and the International Visegrad Fund. The platform also functions as a bridge between V4 and non-V4 experts, offering visiting fellowships to researchers from outside the region.

For 2026, eight visiting fellowships will be awarded across the core network of think tanks that constitute the Think Visegrad platform. Each selected fellow will be hosted by one of these institutions, gaining direct access to the host institute’s research environment, expert networks, and ongoing activities.

Fellowship Duration and Format

The duration of each fellowship is flexible, ranging from 6 to 8 weeks. However, all fellows are required to spend a minimum of 4 weeks in-person at the hosting institution. The exact schedule and balance between in-person and remote work is determined through agreement with the host institute. This hybrid structure allows fellows to immerse themselves in the local policy environment while maintaining flexibility for their ongoing professional commitments.

Financial Support and Coverage

Selected fellows receive comprehensive financial support to ensure a productive and stress-free research stay. The breakdown of funding is as follows:

  • Lump sum of €3,400– This amount is provided to cover all necessary costs and expenditures related to the fellow’s stay at the hosting institute, including:
    • Lodging / accommodation
    • Local transportation
    • Daily living expenses
    • Health and social insurance (fellow’s responsibility)
    • Any applicable taxes and related fees
  • Additional travel reimbursement of up to €500– Think Visegrad covers the cost of international travel from the fellow’s home country to the hosting institute. This reimbursement is provided separately and can be claimed up to the maximum amount of €500.

Fellows should note that they are personally responsible for paying all taxes, fees, and insurance premiums (including health and social insurance) from the €3,400 lump sum. It is strongly recommended that applicants budget accordingly and consult local regulations regarding short-term research stays.

Research Topics and Focus Areas

Fellowships are available across a broad spectrum of research areas within:

  • Political science
  • International relations
  • Economics

However, selected topics must complement the existing research focus of the hosting institute. Therefore, applicants are highly recommended to visit the website of their preferred host institute before submitting an application. Understanding the institute’s ongoing projects, publications, and strategic priorities will significantly strengthen the relevance and competitiveness of your proposal.

Each fellow is expected to achieve two primary deliverables during their fellowship period:

  1. A policy paper– A substantive, evidence-based document addressing a pre-agreed topic relevant to the Visegrad region and the host institute’s expertise.
  2. One public presentation– A seminar, lecture, or workshop delivered at the hosting institute, allowing the fellow to share their findings with local researchers, policymakers, and the broader expert community.

Eligibility Criteria

Before applying, please ensure you meet the following requirements:

  • You must be an expert fellow from a non-Visegrad country(i.e., not a citizen or permanent resident of Czech Republic, Hungary, Poland, or Slovakia).
  • Priority is given to applicants with a proven track record of significant contribution in their field of research.
  • High-quality project proposals from junior and mid-career researchersare also duly considered.
  • Previous Think Visegrad Fellowsare not eligible to apply again. Applications from past fellows will be automatically dismissed without review.

There is no strict age limit or minimum degree requirement, although a strong academic or professional background in political science, international relations, economics, or a closely related discipline is expected.

Host Institutions (Core Network Think Tanks)

The eight visiting fellowships are distributed across the core network think tanks that coordinate the Think Visegrad platform. While the specific list of host institutions for 2026 should be confirmed on the official website, the core network traditionally includes leading V4 research centers with coordinating abilities. The platform remains open to cooperation with other think tanks from all V4 countries, but the 2026 fellowships will be hosted exclusively by members of this core network.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to research each host institute’s thematic focus, ongoing projects, and available mentorship before indicating preferences in their application.

Application Requirements

All application dossiers must be submitted as three separate PDF files. Incomplete applications or files submitted in other formats may not be considered.

The required documents are:

  1. Application form– The official Think Visegrad Fellowship application form (available for download from the program’s website).
  2. Structured CV– A detailed curriculum vitae that includes a complete list of publications, previous research positions, academic degrees, and relevant professional experience. Use clear headings and chronological organization.
  3. Motivation letter– Maximum 300 words. This letter should explain:
    • Why you are interested in the Think Visegrad Fellowship
    • How your research aligns with the host institute’s focus
    • What policy paper topic you propose to develop during the fellowship
    • What you hope to gain from and contribute to the Visegrad research community

All three PDF files must be sent by email to the following address:

thinktank@sfpa.sk

Application Deadline

The deadline for submission is May 15, 2026. Applications received after this date will not be considered. Late or incomplete submissions will be automatically disqualified.

Selection Timeline and Process

While the official selection timeline is not published in the call, applicants should expect the following general process:

  • Initial eligibility screening
  • Review of project proposals and CVs by the core network think tanks
  • Potential interviews (virtual or in-person) with shortlisted candidates
  • Final selection and matching with host institutes
  • Notification of results (likely by mid-to-late 2026, given the Summer/Autumn/Winter fellowship periods)

Fellowships are available for three seasonal periods in 2026:

  • Summer 2026
  • Autumn 2026
  • Winter 2026

Selected fellows will work with their host institute to determine exact start and end dates within the agreed seasonal window.

Why Apply for the Think Visegrad Fellowship?

This fellowship offers more than financial support. It provides:

  • Access to one of Central Europe’s most influential think tank networks
  • An opportunity to publish a policy paper with regional visibility
  • A platform to present your research to V4 policymakers and experts
  • Networking with eight core V4 think tanks and the International Visegrad Fund
  • A chance to understand the political, economic, and security dynamics of the Visegrad region from the inside

For researchers looking to expand their policy impact, build cross-border collaborations, and gain firsthand experience in a strategically important European region, the Think Visegrad Fellowship is an exceptional opportunity.

Important Notes for Applicants

  • Do not submit your application to individual host institutes. All applications must go through the central email: thinktank@sfpa.sk
  • Ensure each of the three PDF files is clearly named (e.g., “LastName_ApplicationForm.pdf”, “LastName_CV.pdf”, “LastName_MotivationLetter.pdf”)
  • The €3,400 lump sum is paid as one amount. Fellows are responsible for all local arrangements including accommodation, insurance, and tax compliance.
  • International travel reimbursement (up to €500) is processed separately. Keep all receipts and travel documentation.
  • You must be available to spend a minimum of 4 continuous weeks in-person at the hosting institute.
  • The fellowship does not cover family members or dependents.

 

Apply here

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Think Visegrad Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Non-V4 Experts at IIR Prague appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants 2026: Funding for High-Impact Journalism Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants 2026 offer a unique and flexible funding opportunity for journalists seeking to produce in-depth, high-impact stories on underreported global issues. Designed to support ambitious reporting projects across all regions and topics, this programme empowers storytellers to bring critical issues to light through innovative and engaging journalism. Open to both […]

The post Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants 2026: Funding for High-Impact Journalism Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants 2026 offer a unique and flexible funding opportunity for journalists seeking to produce in-depth, high-impact stories on underreported global issues. Designed to support ambitious reporting projects across all regions and topics, this programme empowers storytellers to bring critical issues to light through innovative and engaging journalism.

Open to both freelance and staff journalists worldwide, these grants provide financial support, editorial backing, and a platform to amplify stories that matter.

About the Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants

The programme is administered by the Pulitzer Center, a globally recognized institution dedicated to supporting quality journalism that addresses systemic challenges and underreported topics.

Unlike many traditional funding opportunities, the Global Reporting Grants are highly flexible. There are no thematic or geographic restrictions, allowing applicants to explore a wide range of issues—from climate change and conflict to governance, health, and social justice.

The initiative encourages bold storytelling that not only informs but also engages audiences through creative and multimedia approaches.

Funding and Financial Support

The Pulitzer Center provides financial support tailored to each project’s needs.

  • Most grants range between USD 5,000 and USD 10,000
  • Funding is based on a detailed and realistic project budget
  • Payments are typically disbursed in two stages:
    • Initial funding upon approval
    • Final payment upon submission of completed work

This structure ensures that journalists have the resources needed to conduct thorough reporting while maintaining accountability for project delivery.

Programme Focus and Supported Formats

The Global Reporting Grants support a wide range of journalistic formats, reflecting the evolving nature of storytelling in the digital age.

Supported formats include:

  • Print and digital journalism
  • Photojournalism
  • Audio and radio storytelling
  • Video reporting and short documentaries

Projects that integrate multimedia elements, data journalism, and innovative storytelling techniques are particularly encouraged. The goal is to produce compelling content that reaches diverse audiences and drives meaningful conversations.

Eligibility Criteria

The programme is open to a broad and diverse group of applicants:

  • Staff journalists and freelance journalists worldwide
  • Writers, photographers, audio producers, and filmmakers
  • Individual applicants or collaborative teams

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • A clear and feasible reporting plan
  • Strong editorial partnerships or publication strategy
  • The ability to deliver impactful, high-quality journalism

Importantly, there are no restrictions on nationality, topic, or geographic focus, making this one of the most accessible global journalism grants available.

Application Process

Applicants are required to submit their proposals through the official Pulitzer Center platform. A strong application should include:

  • A detailed project proposal
  • A realistic and itemized budget
  • Evidence of editorial partnerships or publishing plans
  • Samples of previous work (if required)

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the official guidelines before submitting their proposals to ensure compliance with all requirements.

Deadline

Deadline: Rolling (applications accepted year-round)

This rolling deadline allows journalists to apply at any time, making it easier to align submissions with project readiness and editorial timelines.

Why Apply for the Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants?

This programme stands out for its flexibility, accessibility, and global reach. It not only provides financial support but also connects journalists with a network of editors, educators, and media partners.

For journalists passionate about uncovering underreported stories and making a real-world impact, the Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants offer a powerful platform to turn ideas into influential journalism.

Start Your Application

If you have a compelling story idea that deserves global attention, this is your opportunity to secure funding and bring it to life. Prepare your proposal, build your editorial partnerships, and submit your application through the official Pulitzer Center platform.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants 2026: Funding for High-Impact Journalism Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
